name: Tex-Mex Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Enriched Hominy Grits (Quaker or Aunt Jemima)
4 oz Monterey jack cheese (with jalapeno peppers, shredded)
1/2 cup Dairy sour half and half
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Lightly grease a cookie sheet.
3. In a large bowl, combine the dry ingredients: flour, grits, baking powder, and salt (if using).
4. Add the margarine or butter to the dry ingredients. Cut in until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
5. Stir in the shredded cheese.
6. In a separate bowl, mix together the sour half and half and milk.
7. Gradually add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients, stirring just until moistened.
8. Shape the dough into a ball and knead gently on a lightly floured surface 10 to 12 times.
9. Roll the dough out to a thickness of 3/4 inch.
10. Using a floured 2-inch biscuit cutter, cut the biscuits and place them 1 inch apart on the prepared cookie sheet.
11. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes or until golden brown.
NUTRITIONAL ANALYSIS per biscuit:
calories: 200
carbohydrates: 18 g
protein: 5 g
fat: 12 g
calcium: 93 mg
sodium: 560 mg
cholesterol: 10 mg
dietary fiber: 0 g
